title: ranunculus ficaria f. disjunctus
intro: Ranunculus ficaria f. disjunctus, also known as lesser celandine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, due to the presence of compounds such as ranunculin and protoanemonin. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Lesser celandine can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used as a natural remedy for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
